---
title: "Genetics Project Progress"
author: "Group Members: Natalie Karten, Lorna Darling, and Brianna Arledge"
format: html
bibliography: Exported Items.bib
csl: nature.csl
---

## Project Goals
Our project aims to write a program to record the voltage output from a scientific instrument as a function of time, and to plot the output in a graph. We will be making three variations of graphs. 

Brianna Arledge's goal is to write the code for the linear variation of the graph.

## Linear Graph
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd 
df = pd.read_csv('Methyl Stearate.xy', sep = r"\s+", engine = "python", header = None)
df.columns = ["Time", "Voltage"]
plt.plot (df["Time"], df["Voltage"])
plt.xlabel("Time")
plt.ylabel("Voltage")
plt.xlim(18, 19)
plt.ylim(46, 56)
plt.title("Methyl Stearate Peak")
plt.show()
```
[@matplotlib_pyplot_2025]

## Bar Graph, this is the graph I plan on coding for - natie karten
```{python}

```

## Surface Area
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load the data
df = pd.read_csv('Methyl Stearate.xy', sep=r"\s+", engine="python", header=None)

# Label columns
df.columns = ["Time", "Voltage"]

# Filter for peak region (18.3 to 18.5)
df_peak = df[(df["Time"] >= 18.3) & (df["Time"] <= 18.5)]

# Compute area under the curve
area = np.trapezoid(df_peak["Voltage"], df_peak["Time"])
print("Area under the curve:", area)

# Plot the data
plt.plot(df_peak["Time"], df_peak["Voltage"], label="Peak")

# Shade the area
plt.fill_between(df_peak["Time"], df_peak["Voltage"], alpha=0.3)

# Label and Format
plt.xlabel("Time")
plt.ylabel("Voltage")
plt.title("Methyl Stearate Peak")

plt.xlim(18, 19)
plt.ylim(46, 56)

plt.legend()
plt.show()
```
